vocab.note: A1 review: combining greeting and question; the standard opening exchange
A2 review: بحب (I like) + Form V verb; combines preference with learning activity

vocab.english: I went and you will come
vocab.note: A2 review: contrasting past and future in one sentence; tests tense mastery

vocab.arabic: ما كُنت عارِف، بَس هَلَّق عارِف
vocab.transcription: mā KUNT ĀR-if, bas ha-LAʔ ĀR-if
vocab.romanization: mā kunt ʕārif, bas hallaʾ ʕārif
vocab.english: I didn't know, but now I know
vocab.note: B1 review: negative past state + contrast with present; typical narrative pivot

vocab.english: If I have time, I will go
vocab.note: B1 review: conditional with future result; key pattern for planning discussions

vocab.english: In my opinion, dignity is most important
vocab.note: B2 review: opinion marker + superlative + abstract concept; combines multiple B-level patterns

options: I will go and you are coming
I went and you will come

quiz.arabic: ما كُنت عارِف، بَس هَلَّق عارِف
quiz.transcription: mā KUNT ĀR-if, bas ha-LAʔ ĀR-if
quiz.romanization: mā kunt ʕārif, bas hallaʾ ʕārif
options: I knew before but not now
I didn't know, but now I know
When I went, there was time
If I have time, I will go
Because I have time, I went
Although I have time, I won't go
In your opinion, dignity is least important
In my opinion, dignity is most important
According to them, dignity is important
In our view, dignity is mentioned

build.english: I went — and then — I came back
If I have time, I will go

speak.scenario: A final comprehensive conversation covering all lesson topics.

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: Hello! How are you?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: Hello, how are you?

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And do you love learning Arabic?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: I love to learn a lot!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: Where did you go today?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: I went and you will come!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: Did you know this before?

turns.speaker: you
turns.arabic: ما كُنت عارِف، بَس هَلَّق عارِف!
turns.romanization: mā kunt ʕārif, bas hallaʾ ʕārif
turns.english: I didn't know, but now I know!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And will you come next week?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: If I have time, I will go!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And what is most important to you?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: In my opinion, dignity is most important!

vocab.english: I wish we had learned more
vocab.note: B2 review: hypothetical + Form V past plural; reflective closing sentiment

vocab.english: from my point of view, the most important
vocab.note: B2 review: formal opinion expression + superlative; complex opinion structure

vocab.english: I enjoyed being with you all
vocab.note: A2–B1 review: past tense of Form VII + plural object pronoun; warm farewell

vocab.arabic: في يَوم مِن الأَيّام، رَح أَروح
vocab.transcription: FĪ YŌM min il-AY-yam, raH a-RŪH
vocab.romanization: fī yōm min il-ʾayyām, raḥ ʾarūḥ
vocab.note: B2 review: hopeful future expression; combines temporal idiom with future tense

vocab.english: in the end, we learned a lot
vocab.note: capstone closing: narrative close + Form V past + adverb; comprehensive wrap-up
capstone closing: combining thank you with Palestinian congratulations phrase

options: I wish we had learned more
from my point of view, most important
I was bored with you all
I enjoyed being with you all

quiz.arabic: في يَوم مِن الأَيّام، رَح أَروح
quiz.transcription: FĪ YŌM min il-AY-yam, raH a-RŪH
quiz.romanization: fī yōm min il-ʾayyām, raḥ ʾarūḥ
options: once upon a time I went
at the beginning we learned little
in the end we learned nothing
in the end, we learned a lot

build.english: I enjoyed being with you all — thank you
In the end — we learned a lot

speak.scenario: You are completing your final Arabic lesson and reflecting on your journey.

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: How was your experience with Arabic?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: I enjoyed being with you all!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And what did you wish?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: I wish we had learned more!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And what's most important to you?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: From my point of view, communication is most important!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And will you communicate in Arabic?

turns.speaker: you
turns.arabic: في يَوم مِن الأَيّام، رَح أَروح!
turns.romanization: fī yōm min il-ʾayyām, raḥ ʾarūḥ
turns.english: One day, I will go!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: And in the end?

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: In the end, we learned a lot!

turns.speaker: them
turns.english: Congratulations on finishing the course!

turns.speaker: you
turns.english: Thank you and congratulations!

all_vocab.english: I went and you will come

all_vocab.arabic: ما كُنت عارِف، بَس هَلَّق عارِف
all_vocab.transcription: mā KUNT ĀR-if, bas ha-LAʔ ĀR-if
all_vocab.romanization: mā kunt ʕārif, bas hallaʾ ʕārif

if I have time, I will go
in my opinion, dignity is most important
I wish we had learned more
from my point of view, most important
I enjoyed being with you all

all_vocab.arabic: في يَوم مِن الأَيّام، رَح أَروح
all_vocab.transcription: FĪ YŌM min il-AY-yam, raH a-RŪH
all_vocab.romanization: fī yōm min il-ʾayyām, raḥ ʾarūḥ

all_vocab.english: in the end, we learned a lot
